summary: Stellar soundboard capture of a marathon Olympia show with awe‑inspiring The Other One and many classic performances, marred only by a brief Casey Jones glitch.
review: The May 1972 Olympia concert is hailed as one of the tour’s top shows, featuring a soaring The Other One sandwiched around Me & My Uncle, a blistering China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider, and relentless energy through two four‑hour sets. The soundboard source is generally clear, though reviewers note a small glitch on Casey Jones that adds a tiny blemish to an otherwise excellent recording.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviews are overwhelmingly positive (5, 5, 5, 4 stars) and praise the musical performance while only flagging a minor technical glitch; recording is a soundboard with good fidelity.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally crisp and well‑balanced, but Casey Jones contains a brief glitch (4:25‑4:30) that some listeners notice.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: A marathon four‑hour set that stays on a high level throughout; the first set is consistently tight while the second maintains momentum with a few looser moments, delivering an overall exhilarating concert experience.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s tone and wah are praised throughout – ‘perfect’ on several cuts, double‑bends on One More Saturday Night, and wild fills that lift Not Fade Away.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil Lesh is highlighted for launching rumble on The Other One and being “ON” during He's Gone, showing tight interplay with the band.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob Weir’s hoarse rasp on Not Fade Away and solid rhythm work are noted positively.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith (keyboard) receives shout‑outs for being ‘ON’ in He's Gone and adding depth to the jams.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann’s 32nd‑note tom rolls and overall drum power receive enthusiastic mention.
